# debtrelieforbankruptcy.com

> Free comparison of all debt relief options vs bankruptcy.

debtrelieforbankruptcy.com is a free, open-source educational resource comparing all debt relief options: consolidation, settlement, credit counseling, debt management plans, Chapter 7, and Chapter 13. Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project.

## Pages

- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/: Debt relief or bankruptcy -- which is right for you? Overview of all options with guidance on when each is appropriate.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/debt-consolidation.html: Debt consolidation explained. How it works, when it helps, and when it makes things worse.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/debt-settlement.html: Debt settlement -- how it works, success rates, and hidden costs.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/credit-counseling.html: Credit counseling and debt management plans (DMPs). How nonprofit agencies work.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/chapter-7.html: Chapter 7 bankruptcy as debt relief. Complete discharge of qualifying debts in 3-4 months.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/chapter-13.html: Chapter 13 as structured debt relief. Court-supervised repayment over 3-5 years.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/comparison-chart.html: Side-by-side comparison of all debt relief options with timelines, costs, and outcomes.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/cost-comparison.html: True cost of each debt relief option including fees, interest, taxes, and credit impact.
- https://debtrelieforbankruptcy.com/faq.html: Frequently asked questions about debt relief vs bankruptcy.

## Key Facts

- Debt consolidation does not reduce what you owe -- it restructures payments, often at lower interest
- Debt settlement typically resolves 25-50% of enrolled debt; the rest accrues interest and fees
- Credit counseling DMPs take 3-5 years and require full repayment at reduced interest
- Chapter 7 eliminates most unsecured debt in 3-4 months with no repayment
- Chapter 13 provides a 3-5 year court-supervised repayment plan
- Settlement companies charge 15-25% of enrolled debt; bankruptcy attorneys charge $1,000-$3,500
- Only bankruptcy provides the automatic stay, stopping lawsuits and garnishments immediately

## Network

Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project:
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener
- 1328f.org -- Research platform
- creditcardforgiveness.org -- Credit card forgiveness options
- debtsettlementvsbankruptcy.com -- Settlement vs bankruptcy comparison
- meanstest.org -- Chapter 7 means test

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
